title.en: Ensure journald log file access is configured

prose.en: Ensure journald log file access is configured

description.en: Journald will create logfiles that do not already exist on the system. This setting controls what permissions will be applied to these newly created files.

rationale.en: It is important to ensure that log files have the correct permissions to ensure that sensitive data is archived and protected.

audit.en: If the default configuration is not appropriate for the site specific requirements, copy `/usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/systemd.conf` to `/etc/tmpfiles.d/systemd.conf` and modify as required. Recommended mode for logfiles is `0640` or more restrictive.

remediation.en: If the default configuration is not appropriate for the site specific requirements, copy `/usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/systemd.conf` to `/etc/tmpfiles.d/systemd.conf` and modify as required. Recommended mode for logfiles is `0640` or more restrictive.

references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: AC-3, AU-2, AU-12, MP-2, SI-5
